description: Hands teaching the dummy reversal technique — ruffing in the long trump hand to generate extra tricks, using dummy's trumps for drawing and control.

hands.title: 241. Basic dummy reversal
hands.notes: Instead of ruffing in dummy, ruff diamonds in hand three times. Dummy's remaining trumps draw the last enemy trumps for ten tricks.

hands.title: 242. Ruff losers in hand instead of dummy
hands.notes: Normally you ruff in the short hand (dummy). Here, reverse the plan: ruff in hand to generate more total tricks than standard play allows.

hands.title: 243. Shorten hand trumps intentionally
hands.notes: Ruff a heart in hand intentionally to shorten your trumps. After three ruffs, dummy has more trumps — dummy draws the remaining trumps.

hands.title: 244. Use dummy's long trumps for control
hands.notes: After shortening your hand by ruffing, use dummy's remaining trumps to draw the opponents' last trumps and maintain control.

hands.title: 245. Count trump length for reversal
hands.notes: Count: you start with five trumps, dummy has three. After ruffing twice in hand, you have three trumps — same as dummy. Verify this is safe.

hands.title: 246. When dummy reversal is superior to normal play
hands.notes: Normal play yields nine tricks. The dummy reversal — ruffing twice in hand and drawing trumps from dummy — produces the vital tenth trick.

hands.title: 247. Entry planning in dummy reversal
hands.notes: Each ruff in hand requires re-entering dummy afterward. Plan entries carefully — the diamond ace and club entries must be timed precisely.

hands.title: 248. Avoid over-shortening the wrong hand
hands.notes: Ruffing too many times in hand can cost control. If you ruff four times, you have only one trump left and defenders may regain the lead. Limit to three ruffs.
